description: Port a representative subset of LEMON's min_cost_flow_test.cc correctness cases to Go.
commit: test: port representative LEMON min_cost_flow_test.cc correctness cases
steps: `TestLEMON_KnownOptimal`: hand-built small graph (4-6 nodes) with documented optimal cost. Assert err==nil, Feasible==true, TotalCost == expected, then checkSolution.
`TestLEMON_FeasibleSupply`: supply reachable via multiple paths; assert Feasible==true and checkSolution.
`TestLEMON_Infeasible_CutSeparated`: src and sink in disjoint weakly-connected components. Assert errors.Is(err, ErrInfeasible).

`TestLEMON_ParallelArcs`: two nodes u,v with three parallel arcs caps [5,5,5] costs [1,2,3], flow demand 12. Assert arc-cost-1 saturates, arc-cost-2 saturates, arc-cost-3 carries 2; TotalCost == 21; checkSolution.
`TestLEMON_NegativeCostArcs`: graph with at least one negative-cost arc but no negative cycle, constructed so optimal necessarily uses it. Assert TotalCost matches hand-computed value; checkSolution.
`TestLEMON_ZeroSupply`: graph where source has only zero-capacity outgoing arcs. Assert TotalFlow == 0, Feasible==true, TotalCost == 0.
acceptance_criteria: lemon_mirror_test.go defines the six tests listed above.
